articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Significant Milestones, Attracts Global Investment
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission reports substantial progress in capacity addition and infrastructure development.
• New investment commitments from international players signal growing confidence in India's green energy transition.
• Focus on developing green hydrogen hubs and establishing supply chains for domestic and export markets.
• Policy incentives like production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF) are driving adoption.
• Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
prelimsPoints: Target year for India to become a green hydrogen hub: 2030.
Key components of the mission include demand creation, production, R&D, and skill development.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in achieving the mission's objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling clarified provisions related to cross-border data transfers and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security.
• Public interest litigation had challenged certain clauses regarding exemptions for government agencies and the composition of the Board.
• The judgment is expected to provide greater clarity and impetus for the implementation of India's robust data protection framework.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions
Indian Constitution
IT and Computers
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and to certain offshore processing activities.
The Data Protection Board of India is established as an independent body for enforcement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the DPDP Act 2023 in safeguarding individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with the right to privacy under the DPDP Act.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the future of data governance and digital economy in India.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books symbolizing a court ruling, with digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

articles.headline: India-Africa Trade Summit Concludes with Renewed Focus on Digital Partnerships and Green Growth
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Africa Trade Summit concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in digital transformation and sustainable development.
• Several MoUs were signed, focusing on technology transfer, skill development, and renewable energy projects.
• India reiterated its commitment to supporting Africa's Agenda 2063 through concessional credit lines and capacity building.
• Discussions included diversification of trade baskets, market access, and addressing global challenges like climate change and food security.
• The summit highlighted India's growing role as a reliable development partner for African nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's engagement with Africa is guided by the 'SAGAR' doctrine (Security And Growth for All in the Region).
Key sectors of cooperation include agriculture, health, education, and infrastructure.
India is one of Africa's largest trading partners and investors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations, particularly in the context of global geopolitical shifts.
Discuss the potential for India-Africa cooperation in achieving sustainable development goals and digital inclusion.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic partnership with African nations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and African flags juxtaposed, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: Africa is home to 54 recognized countries and is the world's second-largest and second-most populous continent.

articles.headline: ISRO Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and high-resolution optical cameras for all-weather imaging capabilities.
• The satellite will significantly bolster India's disaster management efforts, providing real-time data for flood mapping, cyclone tracking, and earthquake damage assessment.
• It also supports agricultural monitoring, urban planning, and coastal zone management.
• This mission underscores India's commitment to leveraging space technology for societal benefit and regional cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are part of ISRO's integrated Earth observation capabilities.
SAR technology allows imaging through clouds and at night.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Analyze how advanced Earth Observation Satellites contribute to sustainable development goals beyond disaster management.
Examine India's growing capabilities in space technology and its implications for regional leadership.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with Earth visible in the background, symbolizing satellite deployment.
articles.trivia: The first Indian satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Millet Cultivation to Enhance Food Security and Farmer Incomes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture announced new initiatives to promote millet cultivation across various states, building on the International Year of Millets 2023 momentum.
• Focus on providing better minimum support prices (MSP), improving access to quality seeds, and promoting millet-based value-added products.
• Millets are recognized for their nutritional benefits, climate resilience, and lower water footprint compared to staple crops like rice and wheat.
• Aims to diversify India's food basket, address malnutrition, and provide sustainable livelihood opportunities for farmers in rain-fed areas.
• Collaboration with FPOs and agri-startups to create robust supply chains and market linkages for millet products.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns
Food processing and related industries
Public Distribution System
Food security
prelimsPoints: India is a major producer of millets globally.
Common millets include Jowar (Sorghum), Bajra (Pearl Millet), Ragi (Finger Millet).
Millets are highly nutritious, rich in fiber, protein, and minerals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of promoting millet cultivation for India's food security, nutritional challenges, and agricultural sustainability.
Analyze the policy measures required to effectively integrate millets into the public distribution system and national dietary guidelines.
Examine how increased millet production can contribute to farmer resilience against climate change and enhance their incomes.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer holding a handful of millets in a field, symbolizing agricultural prosperity.
articles.trivia: The United Nations declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets, following a proposal by India.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focuses on Interoperability Standards
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) revealed significant progress in creating digital health IDs (ABHA) and linking health records.
• The mission is now prioritizing the establishment of robust interoperability standards to ensure seamless exchange of health data across various healthcare providers.
• Challenges in digital literacy and infrastructure in remote areas are being addressed through targeted outreach and capacity building programs.
• Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem that supports universal health coverage and improves access to quality healthcare services.
• Collaboration with private sector healthcare providers and health tech startups is crucial for wider adoption and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Government policies and interventions
IT and Computers
prelimsPoints: ABDM is a flagship initiative launched by the Government of India.
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare, connecting patients, doctors, and other healthcare stakeholders.
ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique identification number for health records.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to transform India's healthcare delivery system and address existing disparities.
Discuss the ethical concerns and data security challenges associated with a centralized digital health ecosystem.
Suggest measures to overcome barriers to adoption and ensure equitable access to digital health services across all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: A network of digital lines connecting various healthcare symbols, representing a digital health ecosystem.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Yojana, launched in 2018, is the world's largest government-funded healthcare program.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-East to Enhance Connectivity and Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated timelines for key border infrastructure projects in India's North-Eastern states.
• Projects include construction of strategic roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems along sensitive international borders.
• Aims to improve connectivity for local populations, facilitate trade, and strengthen national security against external threats and illegal activities.
• Emphasis on integrating local communities into development initiatives and promoting economic opportunities in border areas.
• This push is part of a broader strategy to secure India's eastern frontiers and foster regional development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Roads
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
prelimsPoints: India shares borders with several countries in the North-East, including China, Myanmar, Bangladesh, and Bhutan.
The Border Area Development Programme (BADP) focuses on balanced development of border areas.
Strategic roads are crucial for rapid troop movement and logistics.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure in India's North-Eastern region for both national security and regional development.
Discuss the challenges faced in infrastructure development in mountainous and remote border areas, and suggest solutions.
Examine how improved connectivity can counter insurgency and promote economic integration of border communities.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed road winding through mountainous terrain in the North-East, with military vehicles.
articles.trivia: The Brahmaputra River, a major trans-boundary river, flows through Tibet, India, and Bangladesh, playing a crucial role in the North-East's geography.

articles.headline: India Submits New UNESCO World Heritage Bid for 'Living Root Bridges' of Meghalaya
articles.explainer:
• India has formally submitted a nomination dossier to UNESCO for the 'Living Root Bridges' (Jingkieng Jri) of Meghalaya to be inscribed as a World Heritage Site.
• These unique bio-engineered structures, created by the Khasi and Jaintia tribes from aerial roots of rubber fig trees, represent exceptional human-environment interaction.
• The bid highlights the indigenous knowledge, sustainable practices, and cultural significance of these natural wonders.
• If inscribed, it would bring global recognition and enhance conservation efforts for these fragile ecosystems and the associated cultural heritage.
• The nomination emphasizes the ongoing living tradition and the need to protect both the natural and cultural landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important Geophysical phenomena
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: Living Root Bridges are found primarily in the state of Meghalaya, North-East India.
They are formed by guiding the aerial roots of Ficus elastica trees across rivers and streams.
UNESCO World Heritage Sites are places of outstanding universal value.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of 'Living Root Bridges' as a unique example of human-environment interaction and indigenous engineering.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of UNESCO World Heritage status for cultural and natural sites in India.
Examine how the preservation of such sites contributes to the understanding of sustainable living practices and traditional knowledge systems.
articles.imageDescription: A photograph of a living root bridge spanning a river in a lush green forest.
articles.trivia: Some of the living root bridges are believed to be over 500 years old and can support the weight of 50 people at a time.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Reiterates Cautious Approach on Wider Digital Rupee Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor stated that while pilot projects for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) - e-Rupee - show promise, a cautious and phased approach is necessary for wider adoption.
• Concerns regarding technological stability, cybersecurity risks, privacy implications, and potential impact on financial stability were highlighted.
• The RBI is closely monitoring global developments in CBDCs and assessing the readiness of India's digital infrastructure.
• Emphasis on public education and stakeholder consultation before a full-scale rollout to ensure smooth transition and acceptance.
• The e-Rupee aims to offer benefits like reduced transaction costs, faster settlements, and enhanced financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The e-Rupee is a legal tender issued by the RBI in digital form.
It is distinct from cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.
Pilots are being conducted for both wholesale and retail segments of e-Rupee.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India.
Analyze the challenges the RBI faces in ensuring widespread adoption and integration of the e-Rupee into the existing financial system.
Examine how the e-Rupee could impact financial inclusion, monetary policy effectiveness, and the banking sector in India.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of Indian currency notes, with 'e-Rupee' overlay, symbolizing a digital economy.
articles.trivia: The Bahamas was the first country to launch a full-fledged CBDC, known as the 'Sand Dollar', in 2020.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for UN Peacekeeping, Focus on Women Peacekeepers
articles.explainer:
• India, a leading contributor to UN Peacekeeping missions, reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security at a UN forum.
• The External Affairs Minister announced new pledges, including increased deployment of women peacekeepers and specialized medical units.
• Emphasized the need for robust mandates, adequate resources, and greater safety for peacekeepers in conflict zones.
• Highlighted India's long-standing tradition of contributing to UN efforts and its dedication to multilateralism.
• Discussions also focused on technological solutions for enhancing peacekeeping effectiveness and addressing emerging threats.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
India and its neighborhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
India has contributed over 2.5 lakh troops to various UN missions over the decades.
The first all-women Formed Police Unit (FPU) was deployed by India to Liberia in 2007.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its contribution to global peace and stability.
Analyze the significance of deploying women peacekeepers in enhancing the effectiveness and legitimacy of UN missions.
Examine the challenges faced by UN Peacekeeping operations in addressing modern conflicts and the reforms required.
articles.imageDescription: Indian peacekeepers in blue helmets standing in formation under the UN flag.
articles.trivia: India has lost more peacekeepers in the line of duty than any other country.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Policy Attracts Further Investment, Boosts Domestic Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT reported significant progress in attracting investment under the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM).
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication units and ATMP (Assembly, Test, Marking, and Packaging) facilities have been approved.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on imported semiconductors, critical for various industries from automotive to defense.
• Focus on skill development, R&D, and creating a robust supply chain ecosystem to support the burgeoning industry.
• This initiative is pivotal for India's aspirations to become a global electronics manufacturing hub and achieve technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: ISM is a specialized and independent business division within Digital India Corporation.
Semiconductors are critical components in almost all electronic devices.
The PLI scheme is a key incentive for attracting investment in semiconductor manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of building a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic growth and national security.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in attracting global players and developing indigenous capabilities in the semiconductor industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies, such as the ISM and PLI schemes, in achieving technological self-reliance in this critical sector.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a semiconductor wafer with microchips, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach over a trillion dollars by 2030.

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Enters Phase II, Targets Sustainable Water Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced the commencement of Phase II of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), focusing on sustainable water source management and greywater treatment.
• While Phase I largely achieved 'Har Ghar Jal' (tap water to every rural household) targets, Phase II emphasizes long-term sustainability and water quality monitoring.
• New guidelines promote community participation, local water conservation efforts, and integration of traditional water harvesting structures.
• Aims to ensure functional tap connections with adequate and prescribed quality of water on a regular and long-term basis.
• Collaboration with Panchayati Raj Institutions and self-help groups for maintenance and operation of water supply systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in 2019.
Its primary objective is to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
The mission is based on a community approach to water and includes extensive information, education, and communication.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of the Jal Jeevan Mission in achieving 'Har Ghar Jal' and discuss the critical importance of its Phase II in ensuring long-term water security.
Analyze the role of community participation and decentralized governance in sustainable water resource management.
Discuss the challenges in maintaining water quality and ensuring equitable access to clean drinking water in rural India.
articles.imageDescription: A tap flowing with clean water in a rural setting, symbolizing access to safe drinking water.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's renewable water resources.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress in Urban Air Quality Improvement, Calls for Sustained Efforts
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) indicates measurable improvements in air quality across several Indian cities, attributing it to concerted government efforts.
• Initiatives like the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), stricter emission norms, and promotion of public transport have shown positive impacts.
• However, the report also warns against complacency, noting that many cities still exceed national ambient air quality standards, particularly during winter months.
• Calls for greater inter-ministerial coordination, adoption of cleaner technologies, and behavioral changes for sustained air quality gains.
• Emphasis on data-driven policy making and public awareness campaigns to foster a cleaner urban environment.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCAP aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (from 2017 levels).
PM2.5 and PM10 are fine particulate matters that can cause severe health issues.
CPCB is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of India's policies and programs, such as NCAP, in addressing urban air pollution.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach required to achieve sustainable improvements in air quality in Indian cities.
Examine the socio-economic and health impacts of air pollution and the challenges in implementing stringent environmental regulations.
articles.imageDescription: A clean city skyline with reduced smog, symbolizing improved air quality.
articles.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the world's most polluted cities, particularly during the post-monsoon and winter months due to a combination of factors.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Judicial Accountability Bill, Aims for Greater Transparency
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament of India passed the Judicial Accountability Bill, 2026, after extensive debates and amendments.
• The bill seeks to establish a robust mechanism for investigating complaints against judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts.
• Key provisions include independent inquiry committees, a transparent complaint process, and measures to ensure accountability without compromising judicial independence.
• Addresses long-standing demands for greater transparency and ethical conduct within the judiciary.
• The bill is expected to strengthen public trust in the judicial system while safeguarding the separation of powers.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: The Indian Constitution provides for the removal of judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts through impeachment.
The bill proposes a mechanism for dealing with complaints of misconduct short of impeachment.
The concept of judicial accountability is crucial for maintaining public faith in the judiciary.
mainsPoints: Analyze the need for a Judicial Accountability Bill in India, balancing the principles of judicial independence and accountability.
Discuss the potential impact of the new law on the functioning of the judiciary and its relationship with other branches of government.
Examine how the bill addresses concerns related to transparency, ethical conduct, and public trust in the Indian judicial system.
articles.imageDescription: A parliament building facade with a gavel in the foreground, symbolizing legislative action on judiciary.
articles.trivia: The first impeachment motion against a Supreme Court judge in India was initiated in 1991 against Justice V. Ramaswami.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Engagement with Arctic Council as Observer, Focus on Climate Research
articles.explainer:
• India participated actively in a high-level meeting of the Arctic Council, reaffirming its commitment as an Observer state.
• Emphasized its growing interest in Arctic research, particularly concerning climate change impacts and sustainable resource management.
• India's 'Arctic Policy' outlines scientific research, climate and environmental protection, economic and human development, and international cooperation.
• The Arctic region is crucial for understanding global climate patterns and holds significant strategic and economic potential.
• India highlighted the need for responsible and collaborative exploration, respecting indigenous communities and environmental safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India received Observer status in the Arctic Council in 2013.
India established its first Arctic research station, 'Himadri', in Svalbard, Norway, in 2008.
The Arctic Council is a high-level intergovernmental forum promoting cooperation in the Arctic region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and scientific significance of India's growing engagement with the Arctic region and its implications for global climate change research.
Discuss the key pillars of India's Arctic Policy and how they align with its national interests and international commitments.
Examine the geopolitical challenges and environmental concerns associated with increased activity in the Arctic region and India's role in promoting sustainable practices.
articles.imageDescription: A research vessel in the Arctic waters with icebergs and scientific instruments.
articles.trivia: The Arctic region is warming at least twice as fast as the global average, a phenomenon known as Arctic amplification.

articles.headline: Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Highlights Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, five years post-launch.
• Significant progress noted in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives (e.g., DIKSHA platform).
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access in remote areas, and teacher training.
• The report emphasizes the need for continued investment in infrastructure, faculty development, and leveraging technology for personalized learning.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century skill requirements and foster holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) is a national platform for teachers and students.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of the National Education Policy 2020 on transforming India's education landscape, highlighting both achievements and persistent challenges.
Discuss how NEP 2020 addresses the need for 21st-century skills and holistic development, and what further steps are required for successful implementation.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of universal foundational literacy and equitable access to quality education under NEP.
articles.imageDescription: Students learning in a modern classroom setting with digital tools, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, recommending a common education structure and national system of education.

articles.headline: Defence Indigenization Roadmap Shows Promising Results, Boosts 'Make in India' in Defence Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence released a report detailing significant progress in the indigenization of defence equipment and platforms.
• Several critical weapons systems and technologies, previously imported, are now being designed and manufactured domestically.
• This aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative and aims to reduce import dependence, save foreign exchange, and create jobs.
• Policy measures like positive indigenization lists, enhanced FDI limits, and preferential procurement from Indian vendors are driving this growth.
• Focus on R&D, innovation from startups, and private sector participation to build a robust domestic defence industrial complex.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: The 'Make in India' initiative extends to critical sectors like defence.
Positive Indigenization Lists identify items that must be procured from domestic sources.
DRDO is India's premier agency for military research and development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic imperative for India to achieve self-reliance in defence production, considering geopolitical realities and economic benefits.
Discuss the key policy initiatives undertaken by the government to boost defence indigenization and their effectiveness.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust domestic defence industrial base and suggest measures to overcome them, including the role of private sector and startups.
articles.imageDescription: Modern Indian fighter jets and tanks manufactured domestically, symbolizing defence self-reliance.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, making indigenization a critical priority.

articles.headline: Report Shows Increased Women's Participation in STEM, Gaps Remain in Leadership Roles
articles.explainer:
• A national report highlights a significant increase in women enrolling and graduating in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) fields in India.
• Government initiatives like 'Vigyan Jyoti' and 'KIRAN' have contributed to encouraging girls' participation from school to higher education.
• However, the report points out persistent challenges in retaining women in STEM careers and achieving equitable representation in senior leadership and research positions.
• Calls for proactive measures like mentorship programs, flexible work policies, and addressing unconscious biases in workplaces.
• Promoting women in STEM is crucial for innovation, economic growth, and achieving gender equality in the workforce.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India has one of the highest proportions of women STEM graduates globally.
Initiatives like 'Vigyan Jyoti' aim to encourage girls to pursue STEM careers.
The 'KIRAN' scheme focuses on women scientists and technologists.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the increasing participation of women in STEM education in India and the persistent challenges in their career progression.
Discuss the socio-economic benefits of achieving gender parity in STEM fields for India's innovation ecosystem and economic growth.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and organizational reforms to promote women's retention and leadership in STEM careers in India.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of women scientists in a lab, symbolizing increased participation in STEM.
articles.trivia: Dr. Anandibai Gopalrao Joshi was one of the first Indian female physicians, graduating in Western medicine in 1886.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Framework Gains Global Recognition
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) framework, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received accolades at an international forum on digital governance.
• Several developing nations are keen to adopt elements of India's DPI model to accelerate their own digital transformation and financial inclusion efforts.
• The framework provides a cost-effective, scalable, and inclusive approach to delivering public services and fostering innovation.
• Discussions focused on open-source technologies, data privacy, and interoperability as key components for successful DPI implementation.
• India reiterated its commitment to sharing its expertise and open-source solutions to support global digital growth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
IT and Computers
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India Stack is a set of APIs that allows governments, businesses, startups, and developers to utilize a unique digital infrastructure to solve India’s hard problems towards presence-less, paperless, and cashless service delivery.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
UPI is a real-time payment system.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) on governance, financial inclusion, and economic development.
Discuss the key features of the India Stack (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) that make it a successful model for digital transformation, especially for developing nations.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and equitable access in the context of DPI, and suggest measures to mitigate them.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, forming a digital network.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a new national scheme focused on promoting climate-resilient agricultural practices across vulnerable regions.
• The scheme provides financial incentives, technical support, and training to farmers for adopting drought-resistant crops, water-efficient irrigation, and soil health management.
• Aims to enhance agricultural productivity and farmer incomes in the face of changing climate patterns and extreme weather events.
• Emphasizes integrated farming systems, agroforestry, and sustainable land management techniques.
• Collaboration with agricultural universities and research institutions for developing climate-smart agricultural technologies.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Agricultural produce, storage, transport and marketing and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: Climate-resilient agriculture aims to achieve food security and development goals in the face of climate change.
It involves adaptive measures to cope with adverse impacts of climate change.
Soil Health Card Scheme assesses nutrient status of farm soil and advises on appropriate fertilizer doses.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative for India to adopt climate-resilient agricultural practices, given the vulnerabilities of its farming sector to climate change.
Analyze the key components of the new scheme and evaluate its potential to enhance farmer incomes and ensure food security.
Examine the challenges in promoting sustainable agricultural practices among small and marginal farmers and suggest policy interventions.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy crops in a field, with advanced irrigation systems visible, symbolizing climate-resilient farming.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change, with agriculture being one of the most affected sectors due to its dependence on monsoons.

highlights: India's Green Hydrogen Mission hits major milestones, attracting significant global investment for a sustainable energy future.
Supreme Court upholds core provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, reinforcing India's data privacy framework.
India-Africa Trade Summit boosts digital partnerships and green growth, strengthening South-South cooperation.
Parliament passes a landmark Judicial Accountability Bill, aiming for greater transparency and trust in the judiciary.
India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) framework, including Aadhaar and UPI, gains international recognition for its inclusive model.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Green Hydrogen Mission is/are correct?
1. Its primary aim is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
3. The mission includes policy incentives like Production Linked Incentives (PLI) and Viability Gap Funding (VGF).

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and export by 2030, utilizing renewable energy for electrolysis. It also incorporates financial incentives like PLI and VGF to boost domestic manufacturing and adoption.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It applies to the processing of both digital and physical personal data within India.
2. The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India as an independent enforcement body.
3. The Supreme Court recently upheld its constitutional validity, particularly regarding cross-border data transfers.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as the DPDP Act 2023 primarily applies to the processing of *digital* personal data. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India, and the Supreme Court has upheld its constitutional validity, including provisions on cross-border data transfers.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to 'Living Root Bridges' (Jingkieng Jri) in Meghalaya, consider the following statements:
1. They are bio-engineered structures created by the Khasi and Jaintia tribes.
2. They are formed from the aerial roots of the Banyan tree (Ficus benghalensis).
3. India has submitted a nomination dossier for them to be inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 and 3 are correct. Living Root Bridges are indeed bio-engineered by the Khasi and Jaintia tribes, and India has submitted a UNESCO World Heritage bid for them. Statement 2 is incorrect; they are formed from the aerial roots of the Rubber Fig tree (Ficus elastica), not the Banyan tree.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following correctly describes the 'e-Rupee' in India?
options: (a) It is a privately issued cryptocurrency similar to Bitcoin.
(b) It is a digital currency issued by commercial banks under RBI's supervision.
(c) It is a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) issued as legal tender by the RBI.
(d) It is a digital token for cross-border remittances only.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) It is a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) issued as legal tender by the RBI.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The e-Rupee is India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) as legal tender. It is distinct from private cryptocurrencies and is not issued by commercial banks, although they may facilitate its distribution.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about India's engagement with the Arctic region:
1. India received Observer status in the Arctic Council in 2013.
2. India's first Arctic research station, 'Himadri', is located in Antarctica.
3. India's 'Arctic Policy' focuses solely on scientific research and climate protection.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; India gained Observer status in the Arctic Council in 2013. Statement 2 is incorrect; 'Himadri' is located in Svalbard, Norway, in the Arctic, not Antarctica. Statement 3 is incorrect; India's Arctic Policy is comprehensive, covering scientific research, climate and environmental protection, economic and human development, and international cooperation, not solely research and climate protection.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic importance of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving energy security and fostering sustainable economic growth. Discuss the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and suggest policy measures to overcome them.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its strategic importance for energy security (reduced import dependence) and sustainable growth (decarbonization, new industries, job creation). Enumerate challenges like high initial costs, technology gaps, infrastructure requirements, and regulatory hurdles. Suggest policy measures like R&D incentives, PLI schemes, green financing, international collaborations, and infrastructure development.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, represents a significant step towards safeguarding individual privacy in the digital age. Critically examine the Act's provisions, particularly in light of the recent Supreme Court ruling, and discuss its implications for citizens' rights and the digital economy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions; Indian Constitution; IT and Computers.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the DPDP Act's purpose and key principles. Discuss its main provisions (data fiduciary, data principal rights, cross-border transfers, Data Protection Board). Analyze the Supreme Court's ruling and its impact on the Act's implementation and interpretation. Discuss implications for citizens' privacy rights (consent, right to access/correction/erasure) and the digital economy (compliance burden, trust, innovation). Also, mention concerns about government exemptions and potential challenges.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for defence indigenization is crucial for national security and economic growth. Evaluate the progress made under the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector and discuss the challenges that still need to be addressed to achieve true self-reliance.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management; Science and Technology; Effects of liberalization on the economy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the concept of defence indigenization and its importance. Discuss government initiatives like the 'Make in India' in defence, positive indigenization lists, enhanced FDI, and DAP. Highlight achievements and progress (e.g., specific platforms, increased domestic procurement). Enumerate persistent challenges such as R&D gaps, lack of critical technology, limited private sector participation, quality control, and export market access. Conclude with suggestions for further strengthening the defence industrial base.

quickBytes.text: India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, primarily governs the processing of digital personal data.
Millets are known as 'Nutri-cereals' and are highly climate-resilient crops.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a 14-digit unique identification number for health records.
The 'Living Root Bridges' of Meghalaya are bio-engineered structures made from Ficus elastica roots.
India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) aims to build a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem.
Jal Jeevan Mission's Phase II focuses on sustainable water source management and greywater treatment.
India received Observer status in the Arctic Council in 2013.
NEP 2020 proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, replacing the 10+2 system.

GS1.headline: India Submits New UNESCO World Heritage Bid for 'Living Root Bridges' of Meghalaya
GS1.explainer:
• India has formally submitted a nomination dossier to UNESCO for the 'Living Root Bridges' (Jingkieng Jri) of Meghalaya to be inscribed as a World Heritage Site.
• These unique bio-engineered structures, created by the Khasi and Jaintia tribes from aerial roots of rubber fig trees, represent exceptional human-environment interaction.
• The bid highlights the indigenous knowledge, sustainable practices, and cultural significance of these natural wonders.
• If inscribed, it would bring global recognition and enhance conservation efforts for these fragile ecosystems and the associated cultural heritage.
• The nomination emphasizes the ongoing living tradition and the need to protect both the natural and cultural landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important Geophysical phenomena
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: Living Root Bridges are found primarily in the state of Meghalaya, North-East India.
They are formed by guiding the aerial roots of Ficus elastica trees across rivers and streams.
UNESCO World Heritage Sites are places of outstanding universal value.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of 'Living Root Bridges' as a unique example of human-environment interaction and indigenous engineering.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of UNESCO World Heritage status for cultural and natural sites in India.
Examine how the preservation of such sites contributes to the understanding of sustainable living practices and traditional knowledge systems.
GS1.imageDescription: A photograph of a living root bridge spanning a river in a lush green forest.
GS1.trivia: Some of the living root bridges are believed to be over 500 years old and can support the weight of 50 people at a time.

GS1.headline: Report Shows Increased Women's Participation in STEM, Gaps Remain in Leadership Roles
GS1.explainer:
• A national report highlights a significant increase in women enrolling and graduating in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) fields in India.
• Government initiatives like 'Vigyan Jyoti' and 'KIRAN' have contributed to encouraging girls' participation from school to higher education.
• However, the report points out persistent challenges in retaining women in STEM careers and achieving equitable representation in senior leadership and research positions.
• Calls for proactive measures like mentorship programs, flexible work policies, and addressing unconscious biases in workplaces.
• Promoting women in STEM is crucial for innovation, economic growth, and achieving gender equality in the workforce.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India has one of the highest proportions of women STEM graduates globally.
Initiatives like 'Vigyan Jyoti' aim to encourage girls to pursue STEM careers.
The 'KIRAN' scheme focuses on women scientists and technologists.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the increasing participation of women in STEM education in India and the persistent challenges in their career progression.
Discuss the socio-economic benefits of achieving gender parity in STEM fields for India's innovation ecosystem and economic growth.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and organizational reforms to promote women's retention and leadership in STEM careers in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A diverse group of women scientists in a lab, symbolizing increased participation in STEM.
GS1.trivia: Dr. Anandibai Gopalrao Joshi was one of the first Indian female physicians, graduating in Western medicine in 1886.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling clarified provisions related to cross-border data transfers and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• Emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security.
• Public interest litigation had challenged certain clauses regarding exemptions for government agencies and the composition of the Board.
• The judgment is expected to provide greater clarity and impetus for the implementation of India's robust data protection framework.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions
Indian Constitution
IT and Computers
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act 2023 replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and to certain offshore processing activities.
The Data Protection Board of India is established as an independent body for enforcement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the DPDP Act 2023 in safeguarding individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with the right to privacy under the DPDP Act.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the future of data governance and digital economy in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books symbolizing a court ruling, with digital data streams in the background.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

GS2.headline: India-Africa Trade Summit Concludes with Renewed Focus on Digital Partnerships and Green Growth
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Africa Trade Summit concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in digital transformation and sustainable development.
• Several MoUs were signed, focusing on technology transfer, skill development, and renewable energy projects.
• India reiterated its commitment to supporting Africa's Agenda 2063 through concessional credit lines and capacity building.
• Discussions included diversification of trade baskets, market access, and addressing global challenges like climate change and food security.
• The summit highlighted India's growing role as a reliable development partner for African nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's engagement with Africa is guided by the 'SAGAR' doctrine (Security And Growth for All in the Region).
Key sectors of cooperation include agriculture, health, education, and infrastructure.
India is one of Africa's largest trading partners and investors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations, particularly in the context of global geopolitical shifts.
Discuss the potential for India-Africa cooperation in achieving sustainable development goals and digital inclusion.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic partnership with African nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and African flags juxtaposed, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: Africa is home to 54 recognized countries and is the world's second-largest and second-most populous continent.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Reach, Focuses on Interoperability Standards
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) revealed significant progress in creating digital health IDs (ABHA) and linking health records.
• The mission is now prioritizing the establishment of robust interoperability standards to ensure seamless exchange of health data across various healthcare providers.
• Challenges in digital literacy and infrastructure in remote areas are being addressed through targeted outreach and capacity building programs.
• Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem that supports universal health coverage and improves access to quality healthcare services.
• Collaboration with private sector healthcare providers and health tech startups is crucial for wider adoption and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Government policies and interventions
IT and Computers
prelimsPoints: ABDM is a flagship initiative launched by the Government of India.
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare, connecting patients, doctors, and other healthcare stakeholders.
ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique identification number for health records.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to transform India's healthcare delivery system and address existing disparities.
Discuss the ethical concerns and data security challenges associated with a centralized digital health ecosystem.
Suggest measures to overcome barriers to adoption and ensure equitable access to digital health services across all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: A network of digital lines connecting various healthcare symbols, representing a digital health ecosystem.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Yojana, launched in 2018, is the world's largest government-funded healthcare program.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for UN Peacekeeping, Focus on Women Peacekeepers
GS2.explainer:
• India, a leading contributor to UN Peacekeeping missions, reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security at a UN forum.
• The External Affairs Minister announced new pledges, including increased deployment of women peacekeepers and specialized medical units.
• Emphasized the need for robust mandates, adequate resources, and greater safety for peacekeepers in conflict zones.
• Highlighted India's long-standing tradition of contributing to UN efforts and its dedication to multilateralism.
• Discussions also focused on technological solutions for enhancing peacekeeping effectiveness and addressing emerging threats.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
India and its neighborhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
India has contributed over 2.5 lakh troops to various UN missions over the decades.
The first all-women Formed Police Unit (FPU) was deployed by India to Liberia in 2007.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its contribution to global peace and stability.
Analyze the significance of deploying women peacekeepers in enhancing the effectiveness and legitimacy of UN missions.
Examine the challenges faced by UN Peacekeeping operations in addressing modern conflicts and the reforms required.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian peacekeepers in blue helmets standing in formation under the UN flag.
GS2.trivia: India has lost more peacekeepers in the line of duty than any other country.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Judicial Accountability Bill, Aims for Greater Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• The Parliament of India passed the Judicial Accountability Bill, 2026, after extensive debates and amendments.
• The bill seeks to establish a robust mechanism for investigating complaints against judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts.
• Key provisions include independent inquiry committees, a transparent complaint process, and measures to ensure accountability without compromising judicial independence.
• Addresses long-standing demands for greater transparency and ethical conduct within the judiciary.
• The bill is expected to strengthen public trust in the judicial system while safeguarding the separation of powers.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: The Indian Constitution provides for the removal of judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts through impeachment.
The bill proposes a mechanism for dealing with complaints of misconduct short of impeachment.
The concept of judicial accountability is crucial for maintaining public faith in the judiciary.
mainsPoints: Analyze the need for a Judicial Accountability Bill in India, balancing the principles of judicial independence and accountability.
Discuss the potential impact of the new law on the functioning of the judiciary and its relationship with other branches of government.
Examine how the bill addresses concerns related to transparency, ethical conduct, and public trust in the Indian judicial system.
GS2.imageDescription: A parliament building facade with a gavel in the foreground, symbolizing legislative action on judiciary.
GS2.trivia: The first impeachment motion against a Supreme Court judge in India was initiated in 1991 against Justice V. Ramaswami.

GS2.headline: India Strengthens Engagement with Arctic Council as Observer, Focus on Climate Research
GS2.explainer:
• India participated actively in a high-level meeting of the Arctic Council, reaffirming its commitment as an Observer state.
• Emphasized its growing interest in Arctic research, particularly concerning climate change impacts and sustainable resource management.
• India's 'Arctic Policy' outlines scientific research, climate and environmental protection, economic and human development, and international cooperation.
• The Arctic region is crucial for understanding global climate patterns and holds significant strategic and economic potential.
• India highlighted the need for responsible and collaborative exploration, respecting indigenous communities and environmental safeguards.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India received Observer status in the Arctic Council in 2013.
India established its first Arctic research station, 'Himadri', in Svalbard, Norway, in 2008.
The Arctic Council is a high-level intergovernmental forum promoting cooperation in the Arctic region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and scientific significance of India's growing engagement with the Arctic region and its implications for global climate change research.
Discuss the key pillars of India's Arctic Policy and how they align with its national interests and international commitments.
Examine the geopolitical challenges and environmental concerns associated with increased activity in the Arctic region and India's role in promoting sustainable practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A research vessel in the Arctic waters with icebergs and scientific instruments.
GS2.trivia: The Arctic region is warming at least twice as fast as the global average, a phenomenon known as Arctic amplification.

GS2.headline: Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Highlights Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, five years post-launch.
• Significant progress noted in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives (e.g., DIKSHA platform).
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access in remote areas, and teacher training.
• The report emphasizes the need for continued investment in infrastructure, faculty development, and leveraging technology for personalized learning.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century skill requirements and foster holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of the 10+2 system.
DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) is a national platform for teachers and students.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of the National Education Policy 2020 on transforming India's education landscape, highlighting both achievements and persistent challenges.
Discuss how NEP 2020 addresses the need for 21st-century skills and holistic development, and what further steps are required for successful implementation.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of universal foundational literacy and equitable access to quality education under NEP.
GS2.imageDescription: Students learning in a modern classroom setting with digital tools, symbolizing education reform.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, recommending a common education structure and national system of education.

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Framework Gains Global Recognition
GS2.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) framework, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received accolades at an international forum on digital governance.
• Several developing nations are keen to adopt elements of India's DPI model to accelerate their own digital transformation and financial inclusion efforts.
• The framework provides a cost-effective, scalable, and inclusive approach to delivering public services and fostering innovation.
• Discussions focused on open-source technologies, data privacy, and interoperability as key components for successful DPI implementation.
• India reiterated its commitment to sharing its expertise and open-source solutions to support global digital growth.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
IT and Computers
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India Stack is a set of APIs that allows governments, businesses, startups, and developers to utilize a unique digital infrastructure to solve India’s hard problems towards presence-less, paperless, and cashless service delivery.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number.
UPI is a real-time payment system.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) on governance, financial inclusion, and economic development.
Discuss the key features of the India Stack (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker) that make it a successful model for digital transformation, especially for developing nations.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and equitable access in the context of DPI, and suggest measures to mitigate them.
GS2.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, forming a digital network.
GS2.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Significant Milestones, Attracts Global Investment
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission reports substantial progress in capacity addition and infrastructure development.
• New investment commitments from international players signal growing confidence in India's green energy transition.
• Focus on developing green hydrogen hubs and establishing supply chains for domestic and export markets.
• Policy incentives like production-linked incentives (PLI) and viability gap funding (VGF) are driving adoption.
• Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
prelimsPoints: Target year for India to become a green hydrogen hub: 2030.
Key components of the mission include demand creation, production, R&D, and skill development.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in achieving the mission's objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: ISRO Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and high-resolution optical cameras for all-weather imaging capabilities.
• The satellite will significantly bolster India's disaster management efforts, providing real-time data for flood mapping, cyclone tracking, and earthquake damage assessment.
• It also supports agricultural monitoring, urban planning, and coastal zone management.
• This mission underscores India's commitment to leveraging space technology for societal benefit and regional cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are part of ISRO's integrated Earth observation capabilities.
SAR technology allows imaging through clouds and at night.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of space technology in enhancing India's disaster preparedness and response mechanisms.
Analyze how advanced Earth Observation Satellites contribute to sustainable development goals beyond disaster management.
Examine India's growing capabilities in space technology and its implications for regional leadership.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with Earth visible in the background, symbolizing satellite deployment.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Millet Cultivation to Enhance Food Security and Farmer Incomes
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture announced new initiatives to promote millet cultivation across various states, building on the International Year of Millets 2023 momentum.
• Focus on providing better minimum support prices (MSP), improving access to quality seeds, and promoting millet-based value-added products.
• Millets are recognized for their nutritional benefits, climate resilience, and lower water footprint compared to staple crops like rice and wheat.
• Aims to diversify India's food basket, address malnutrition, and provide sustainable livelihood opportunities for farmers in rain-fed areas.
• Collaboration with FPOs and agri-startups to create robust supply chains and market linkages for millet products.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns
Food processing and related industries
Public Distribution System
Food security
prelimsPoints: India is a major producer of millets globally.
Common millets include Jowar (Sorghum), Bajra (Pearl Millet), Ragi (Finger Millet).
Millets are highly nutritious, rich in fiber, protein, and minerals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of promoting millet cultivation for India's food security, nutritional challenges, and agricultural sustainability.
Analyze the policy measures required to effectively integrate millets into the public distribution system and national dietary guidelines.
Examine how increased millet production can contribute to farmer resilience against climate change and enhance their incomes.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer holding a handful of millets in a field, symbolizing agricultural prosperity.
GS3.trivia: The United Nations declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets, following a proposal by India.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-East to Enhance Connectivity and Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated timelines for key border infrastructure projects in India's North-Eastern states.
• Projects include construction of strategic roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems along sensitive international borders.
• Aims to improve connectivity for local populations, facilitate trade, and strengthen national security against external threats and illegal activities.
• Emphasis on integrating local communities into development initiatives and promoting economic opportunities in border areas.
• This push is part of a broader strategy to secure India's eastern frontiers and foster regional development.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Roads
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
prelimsPoints: India shares borders with several countries in the North-East, including China, Myanmar, Bangladesh, and Bhutan.
The Border Area Development Programme (BADP) focuses on balanced development of border areas.
Strategic roads are crucial for rapid troop movement and logistics.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure in India's North-Eastern region for both national security and regional development.
Discuss the challenges faced in infrastructure development in mountainous and remote border areas, and suggest solutions.
Examine how improved connectivity can counter insurgency and promote economic integration of border communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed road winding through mountainous terrain in the North-East, with military vehicles.
GS3.trivia: The Brahmaputra River, a major trans-boundary river, flows through Tibet, India, and Bangladesh, playing a crucial role in the North-East's geography.

GS3.headline: RBI Governor Reiterates Cautious Approach on Wider Digital Rupee Adoption
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor stated that while pilot projects for the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) - e-Rupee - show promise, a cautious and phased approach is necessary for wider adoption.
• Concerns regarding technological stability, cybersecurity risks, privacy implications, and potential impact on financial stability were highlighted.
• The RBI is closely monitoring global developments in CBDCs and assessing the readiness of India's digital infrastructure.
• Emphasis on public education and stakeholder consultation before a full-scale rollout to ensure smooth transition and acceptance.
• The e-Rupee aims to offer benefits like reduced transaction costs, faster settlements, and enhanced financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The e-Rupee is a legal tender issued by the RBI in digital form.
It is distinct from cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.
Pilots are being conducted for both wholesale and retail segments of e-Rupee.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India.
Analyze the challenges the RBI faces in ensuring widespread adoption and integration of the e-Rupee into the existing financial system.
Examine how the e-Rupee could impact financial inclusion, monetary policy effectiveness, and the banking sector in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital representation of Indian currency notes, with 'e-Rupee' overlay, symbolizing a digital economy.
GS3.trivia: The Bahamas was the first country to launch a full-fledged CBDC, known as the 'Sand Dollar', in 2020.

GS3.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Policy Attracts Further Investment, Boosts Domestic Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and IT reported significant progress in attracting investment under the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM).
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication units and ATMP (Assembly, Test, Marking, and Packaging) facilities have been approved.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on imported semiconductors, critical for various industries from automotive to defense.
• Focus on skill development, R&D, and creating a robust supply chain ecosystem to support the burgeoning industry.
• This initiative is pivotal for India's aspirations to become a global electronics manufacturing hub and achieve technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: ISM is a specialized and independent business division within Digital India Corporation.
Semiconductors are critical components in almost all electronic devices.
The PLI scheme is a key incentive for attracting investment in semiconductor manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of building a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic growth and national security.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in attracting global players and developing indigenous capabilities in the semiconductor industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies, such as the ISM and PLI schemes, in achieving technological self-reliance in this critical sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of a semiconductor wafer with microchips, symbolizing advanced manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach over a trillion dollars by 2030.

GS3.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Enters Phase II, Targets Sustainable Water Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced the commencement of Phase II of the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), focusing on sustainable water source management and greywater treatment.
• While Phase I largely achieved 'Har Ghar Jal' (tap water to every rural household) targets, Phase II emphasizes long-term sustainability and water quality monitoring.
• New guidelines promote community participation, local water conservation efforts, and integration of traditional water harvesting structures.
• Aims to ensure functional tap connections with adequate and prescribed quality of water on a regular and long-term basis.
• Collaboration with Panchayati Raj Institutions and self-help groups for maintenance and operation of water supply systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in 2019.
Its primary objective is to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
The mission is based on a community approach to water and includes extensive information, education, and communication.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of the Jal Jeevan Mission in achieving 'Har Ghar Jal' and discuss the critical importance of its Phase II in ensuring long-term water security.
Analyze the role of community participation and decentralized governance in sustainable water resource management.
